protocol_definition.name: Voting
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents answer independently, then cast private ballots; plurality selects the output.
protocol_definition.visibility: Answers are private before voting; voters see all anonymized proposals during voting.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after ballot aggregation; deterministic lowest-index tie break.

1. Executive summary
This plan outlines a 12-week pilot launch for an AI course-QA assistant serving two undergraduate courses (~300 students). With a $5,000 budget, a part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, and one teaching assistant, the assistant will answer questions only from instructor-approved materials, with safeguards against hallucination, privacy breaches, academic-integrity issues, and escalation to human staff. The pilot is structured in four phases: foundation, development, testing, and live deployment with evaluation. A go/no-go decision gate at week 12 determines whether to expand. Assumptions are clearly labeled throughout.

2. Goals and non-goals
Goals:
- Deploy a functional AI assistant that answers course-specific questions using only instructor-approved materials (syllabi, lecture notes, readings, assignment rubrics).
- Achieve ≥80% student satisfaction (survey) and ≥70% question-answer accuracy (human review of a sample).
- Ensure zero reported privacy violations or academic-integrity breaches during the pilot.
- Establish an escalation process that routes unanswered or sensitive questions to human staff within 4 hours.
- Complete the pilot within the $5,000 budget.

Non-goals:
- Replace human teaching assistants or faculty office hours.
- Answer questions outside instructor-approved materials.
- Provide personalized tutoring or grading.
- Scale to other courses or departments during the 12-week pilot.
- Achieve perfect accuracy; the assistant is a supplement, not a sole source.

3. Stakeholder map
| Stakeholder | Role | Interest | Engagement |
|-------------|------|----------|------------|
| Faculty sponsor (part-time) | Project owner, approves materials, reviews safeguards | Ensuring academic integrity and quality | Weekly check-ins, final sign-off |
| Two student developers | Build and maintain the AI assistant | Technical implementation, learning experience | Daily stand-ups, sprint reviews |
| Teaching assistant (TA) | Human escalation point, reviews flagged questions | Workload management, student support | Daily monitoring, escalation handling |
| Students (300) | End users | Quick, accurate answers to course questions | Surveys, usage analytics, feedback |
| Department head | Approves expansion | Strategic alignment, resource allocation | Monthly briefings, final go/no-go decision |
| IT/legal (consulted) | Advise on privacy and data security | Compliance with university policies | Ad-hoc as needed (assumed no formal policy changes required) |

4. 12-week roadmap table with phases, tasks, owners, and deliverables
| Phase | Week | Tasks | Owner | Deliverables |
|-------|------|-------|-------|--------------|
| Foundation | 1-2 | Define approved materials list; set up data storage (e.g., vector database); draft privacy and academic-integrity safeguards; create escalation protocol | Faculty sponsor, student developers | Approved materials inventory; safeguard documentation; escalation flowchart |
| Development | 3-5 | Build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline; integrate hallucination guardrails (e.g., confidence threshold, source citation); develop student-facing interface (e.g., chatbot widget) | Student developers | Working prototype; interface mockup; guardrail test results |
| Testing | 6-8 | Internal testing with TA and faculty; refine accuracy and escalation triggers; conduct small-scale user testing with 20 volunteer students; fix bugs | Student developers, TA, faculty sponsor | Test report; bug fixes; user feedback summary |
| Deployment | 9-12 | Launch to all 300 students; monitor usage and accuracy; collect survey data; hold weekly review meetings; prepare expansion report | All stakeholders | Live assistant; weekly dashboards; student survey results; go/no-go recommendation |

5. Resource and budget allocation
| Item | Cost | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Cloud hosting (e.g., API calls, vector DB) | $1,500 | Assumes 300 students × ~50 queries each over 4 weeks |
| LLM API usage (e.g., GPT-4 or open-source) | $2,000 | Capped at $500/week during deployment; use cheaper model for testing |
| Student developer stipends (2 × $500) | $1,000 | Assumes part-time, 10 hours/week for 6 weeks |
| TA overtime (10 hours) | $300 | For escalation and testing support |
| Contingency

1. Executive summary
This plan outlines a 12-week pilot launch for an AI course-QA assistant serving two undergraduate courses (~300 students). With a $5,000 budget, a part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, and one teaching assistant, the assistant will answer questions strictly from instructor-approved materials, with safeguards against hallucination, privacy breaches, academic-integrity violations, and escalation to human staff. The pilot proceeds through four phases: foundation, development, testing, and live operation. Success is measured by answer accuracy, student satisfaction, and staff workload impact. A go/no-go decision at week 12 determines whether to expand.

2. Goals and non-goals
Goals:
- Deploy a functional AI assistant that answers course-content questions using only instructor-approved materials.
- Achieve ≥85% answer accuracy (verified by TA review) by week 10.
- Maintain zero privacy or academic-integrity incidents during the pilot.
- Collect sufficient usage and satisfaction data to inform expansion decisions.
- Stay within $5,000 budget.

Non-goals:
- Answering questions outside approved materials (e.g., personal advice, non-course topics).
- Replacing human staff (TA, faculty) for complex or sensitive issues.
- Achieving full automation of all student queries.
- Scaling beyond two courses during the pilot.

3. Stakeholder map
| Stakeholder | Role | Interest | Influence |
|-------------|------|----------|-----------|
| Faculty sponsor (part-time) | Project owner, approves materials, final escalation point | High | High |
| Two student developers | Build and maintain assistant | High | Medium |
| Teaching assistant (TA) | Reviews answers, handles escalations, collects metrics | High | Medium |
| Students (300) | End users | Medium | Low (feedback) |
| Department chair | Approves expansion, provides oversight | Low | High |
| IT/security office | Ensures data privacy and system compliance | Low | Medium |
| University legal/compliance | Reviews academic-integrity safeguards | Low | Medium |

4. 12-week roadmap table with phases, tasks, owners, and deliverables
| Phase | Week | Tasks | Owner | Deliverables |
|-------|------|-------|-------|--------------|
| Foundation | 1 | Define scope, select AI platform (e.g., open-source LLM with RAG), set up project repo | Faculty sponsor, student developers | Scope document, platform selection memo |
| Foundation | 2 | Collect and digitize instructor-approved materials (syllabi, lecture notes, readings) | Faculty sponsor, TA | Approved materials repository (PDF/text) |
| Foundation | 3 | Design safeguards: hallucination detection, privacy filters, academic-integrity disclaimers, escalation triggers | Student developers | Safeguard design document |
| Development | 4 | Build RAG pipeline: ingest approved materials, create vector database | Student developers | Working RAG prototype |
| Development | 5 | Implement answer generation with guardrails (e.g., confidence threshold, source citation) | Student developers | Guardrail-integrated assistant |
| Development | 6 | Build escalation interface: flag uncertain/off-topic queries to TA | Student developers, TA | Escalation dashboard |
| Testing | 7 | Internal testing: student developers and TA test with sample queries | Student developers, TA | Test report with accuracy metrics |
| Testing | 8 | Faculty sponsor reviews answers, approves materials updates | Faculty sponsor | Approval sign-off |
| Testing | 9 | Soft launch to 50 volunteer students; collect feedback | TA, student developers | Soft-launch feedback report |
| Live operation | 10 | Full launch to 300 students; monitor usage and accuracy | TA, student developers | Live monitoring dashboard |
| Live operation | 11 | Mid-pilot review: adjust safeguards, update materials, address issues | Faculty sponsor, TA | Mid-pilot adjustment log |
| Live operation | 12 | Final data collection, stakeholder survey, prepare expansion recommendation | Faculty sponsor, TA | Final pilot report |

5. Resource and budget allocation
| Item | Cost | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Cloud compute for RAG and LLM (12 weeks) | $2,000 | Use cost-efficient GPU instances; assume $200/week for 10 weeks of active development/operation |
| Vector database hosting | $500 | Managed service (e.g., Pinecone free tier + small paid upgrade) |
| Student developer stipends (2 students, 10 hrs/week each, 12 weeks) | $1,200 | $5/hr (university rate) – total 240 hours |
| TA overtime (5 hrs/week extra, 12 weeks) | $600 | $10/hr |
| Miscellaneous (survey tools, printing, contingency) | $700 | Buffer for unexpected costs |
| **Total** | **$5,000** | |

Assumption: Faculty sponsor and TA base salaries are covered by existing university funding; only additional stipend/overtime is charged to pilot budget.

6. Risk register with mitigations
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| AI hallucination (incorrect answer) | Medium | High | Use RAG with source citation; set confidence threshold; TA reviews flagged answers |
| Privacy breach (student data exposed) | Low | High | No PII stored; assistant logs anonymized; IT security review before launch |
| Academic-integrity violation (students use assistant to cheat) | Medium | Medium | Assistant only answers from approved materials; disclaimers added; faculty monitors for misuse |
| Low student adoption | Medium | Medium | Soft launch with incentives (e.g., extra credit for feedback); promote via course announcements |
| Budget overrun | Low | Medium | Weekly budget tracking; use free tiers where possible; contingency fund |
| Technical failure (downtime, latency) | Medium | Low | Redundant cloud instances; fallback to TA email for escalations |

7. Success metrics and data collection plan
Metrics:
- Answer accuracy: % of answers verified correct by TA (target ≥85% by week 10). Collected via weekly random sample of 50 answers.
- Student satisfaction: average rating (1-5) from end-of-pilot survey (target ≥4.0).
- Escalation rate: % of queries escalated to TA (target ≤15%).
- Usage rate: % of students who use assistant at least once (target ≥60%).
- Privacy/academic-integrity incidents: count (target 0).

Data collection:
- Automated logging of queries, answers, confidence scores, escalations.
- Weekly TA review logs.
- Student survey at week 12 (anonymous, optional).
- Faculty sponsor observation notes.

8. Governance and escalation process
- Daily: Student developers monitor system logs; TA reviews flagged answers.
- Weekly: TA and student developers meet to review metrics; escalate to faculty sponsor if accuracy <80% or incidents occur.
- Bi-weekly: Faculty sponsor reviews progress, approves material updates.
- Escalation path: Student query → AI assistant → (if uncertain/off-topic) → TA → (if unresolved) → Faculty sponsor.
- Decision gates: Week 6 (continue to testing if prototype passes internal tests), Week 9 (continue to full launch if soft launch accuracy ≥80%).

9. Go / no-go decision criteria for expansion
Go criteria (all must be met):
- Answer accuracy ≥85% in final week.
- Student satisfaction rating ≥4.0/5.0.
- Zero privacy or academic-integrity incidents.
- Escalation rate ≤15%.
- Usage rate ≥60%.
- Budget spent ≤$5,000.

No-go criteria (any one triggers no expansion):
- Accuracy <80%.
- Any privacy or academic-integrity incident.
- Student satisfaction <3.5.
- Budget overrun >10%.

If no-go: Document lessons learned, recommend improvements, and propose a revised pilot with additional safeguards or resources.

10. Final recommendation
Proceed with the 12-week pilot as outlined. The plan is conservative, budget-constrained, and focused on safety and evidence-based decision-making. By restricting answers to instructor-approved materials and embedding multiple safeguards, the pilot minimizes risk while generating actionable data. The go/no-go criteria ensure that expansion only occurs if the assistant demonstrably meets quality, safety, and adoption thresholds. If successful, the pilot can scale to additional courses with a proven model.

This plan outlines a 12-week pilot launch for an AI course-QA assistant supporting two undergraduate courses (≈300 students). Success metrics focus on answer accuracy, student satisfaction, and staff workload impact. A go/no-go decision at week 12 determines expansion.

2. Goals and non-goals
Goals:
- Deploy a functional AI assistant that answers questions solely from instructor-approved course materials.
- Achieve ≥90% answer accuracy (verified by TA) on a test set of 50 common questions per course.
- Collect usage data and student/staff feedback to inform expansion.
- Stay within $5,000 budget and complete within 12 weeks.

Non-goals:
- Do not create new university policies or assume prior pilot results.
- Do not allow the assistant to answer from external or unapproved sources.
- Do not replace human staff; escalation to TA/faculty remains mandatory for complex or sensitive queries.

3. Stakeholder map
| Stakeholder | Role | Interest |
|-------------|------|----------|
| Faculty sponsor (part-time) | Project owner, approves course materials, final decision on expansion | Academic integrity, student learning |
| Student developers (2) | Build and maintain assistant | Technical implementation, timeline |

| Students (≈300) | End users | Quick, accurate answers |

| IT/legal (consulted) | Advise on privacy and data security | Compliance |

| Phase | Week(s) | Tasks | Owner | Deliverables |
|-------|---------|-------|-------|--------------|

| Testing | 6–8 | TA runs 50 test questions per course; measure accuracy; fix bugs; conduct small user test (10 students) | TA, student developers | Accuracy report; bug list; user feedback summary |
| Live pilot | 9–11 | Deploy to all 300 students; monitor usage; collect feedback via survey; handle escalations | TA, faculty sponsor | Usage logs; survey results; escalation log |
| Review | 12 | Analyze metrics; prepare go/no-go report; present to faculty sponsor | Faculty sponsor, TA | Decision report; recommendation |

| Item | Cost | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| AI platform hosting (e.g., cloud GPU for RAG) | $2,000 | 12 weeks, low-traffic estimate |
| Student developer stipends (2 × $1,000) | $2,000 | Part-time, 12 weeks |
| TA overtime (10 hours) | $500 | For testing and escalation |
| Survey tool / misc. | $500 | e.g., Qualtrics license, printing |
| **Total** | **$5,000** | Within budget |

Assumption: Faculty sponsor and TA are already paid by university; no additional salary cost.

6. Risk register with mitigations
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| AI hallucinates answers | Medium | High | Use RAG with strict source retrieval; confidence threshold; TA reviews all answers in test phase |
| Low student adoption | Medium | Medium | Announce via course page; offer incentive (e.g., early access to answers) |
| Privacy breach (e.g., student data leaked) | Low | High | No PII stored; logs anonymized; IT review before launch |
| Budget overrun | Low | Medium | Use free-tier cloud credits; cap hosting costs |
| Faculty sponsor unavailable | Low | Medium | Schedule weekly 30-min check-in; TA as backup decision-maker |
| Escalation overloads TA | Medium | Medium | Set clear escalation criteria; monitor volume; adjust if needed |

7. Success metrics and data collection plan
| Metric | Target | Data source | Collection method |
|--------|--------|-------------|-------------------|
| Answer accuracy | ≥90% on test set | TA test results | Week 7 report |
| Student satisfaction | ≥3.5/5 on survey | End-of-pilot survey | Week 11 survey |
| Usage rate | ≥50% of students use at least once | Server logs | Weekly dashboard |
| Escalation rate | ≤10% of queries | Escalation log | Weekly review |
| Staff time saved | TA reports reduced repetitive questions | TA self-report | Week 12 interview |

8. Governance and escalation process
- Weekly 30-min check-in: faculty sponsor, one student developer, TA.

- Decision gates: End of week 4 (prototype approval), week 8 (go for live pilot), week 12 (expansion decision).
- Privacy: All logs anonymized; no student names or IDs stored. Data deleted after 30 days.

If successful, the pilot can scale to additional courses with a proven model.

| Live operation | 11 | Mid-pilot review: adjust safeguards, update materials, address issues | Faculty sponsor, TA | Mid-pilot adjustment log |
| Live operation | 12 | Final data collection, stakeholder survey, prepare expansion recommendation | Faculty sponsor, TA | Final pilot report |

5. Resource and budget allocation
| Item | Cost | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| Cloud compute for RAG and LLM (12 weeks) | $2,000 | Use cost-efficient GPU instances; assume $200/week for 10 weeks of active development/operation |
| Vector database hosting | $500 | Managed service (e.g., Pinecone free tier + small paid upgrade) |
| Student developer stipends (2 students, 10 hrs/week each, 12 weeks) | $1,200 | $5/hr (university rate) – total 240 hours |
| TA overtime (5 hrs/week extra, 12 weeks) | $600 | $10/hr |
| Miscellaneous (survey tools, printing, contingency) | $700 | Buffer for unexpected costs |
| **Total** | **$5,000** | |

Assumption: Faculty sponsor and TA base salaries are covered by existing university funding; only additional stipend/overtime is charged to pilot budget.

6. Risk register with mitigations
| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|------------|
| AI hallucination (incorrect answer) | Medium | High | Use RAG with source citation; set confidence threshold; TA reviews flagged answers |
| Privacy breach (student data exposed) | Low | High | No PII stored; assistant logs anonymized; IT security review before launch |
| Academic-integrity violation (students use assistant to cheat) | Medium | Medium | Assistant only answers from approved materials; disclaimers added; faculty monitors for misuse |
| Low student adoption | Medium | Medium | Soft launch with incentives (e.g., extra credit for feedback); promote via course announcements |
| Budget overrun | Low | Medium | Weekly budget tracking; use free tiers where possible; contingency fund |
| Technical failure (downtime, latency) | Medium | Low | Redundant cloud instances; fallback to TA email for escalations |

7. Success metrics and data collection plan
Metrics:
- Answer accuracy: % of answers verified correct by TA (target ≥85% by week 10). Collected via weekly random sample of 50 answers.
- Student satisfaction: average rating (1-5) from end-of-pilot survey (target ≥4.0).
- Escalation rate: % of queries escalated to TA (target ≤15%).
- Usage rate: % of students who use assistant at least once (target ≥60%).
- Privacy/academic-integrity incidents: count (target 0).

Data collection:
- Automated logging of queries, answers, confidence scores, escalations.
- Weekly TA review logs.
- Student survey at week 12 (anonymous, optional).
- Faculty sponsor observation notes.

8. Governance and escalation process
- Daily: Student developers monitor system logs; TA reviews flagged answers.
- Weekly: TA and student developers meet to review metrics; escalate to faculty sponsor if accuracy <80% or incidents occur.
- Bi-weekly: Faculty sponsor reviews progress, approves material updates.
- Escalation path: Student query → AI assistant → (if uncertain/off-topic) → TA → (if unresolved) → Faculty sponsor.
- Decision gates: Week 6 (continue to testing if prototype passes internal tests), Week 9 (continue to full launch if soft launch accuracy ≥80%).

9. Go / no-go decision criteria for expansion
Go criteria (all must be met):
- Answer accuracy ≥85% in final week.
- Student satisfaction rating ≥4.0/5.0.
- Zero privacy or academic-integrity incidents.
- Escalation rate ≤15%.
- Usage rate ≥60%.
- Budget spent ≤$5,000.

No-go criteria (any one triggers no expansion):
- Accuracy <80%.
- Any privacy or academic-integrity incident.
- Student satisfaction <3.5.
- Budget overrun >10%.

If no-go: Document lessons learned, recommend improvements, and propose a revised pilot with additional safeguards or resources.

10. Final recommendation
Proceed with the 12-week pilot as outlined. The plan is conservative, budget-constrained, and focused on safety and evidence-based decision-making. By restricting answers to instructor-approved materials and embedding multiple safeguards, the pilot minimizes risk while generating actionable data. The go/no-go criteria ensure that expansion only occurs if the assistant demonstrably meets quality, safety, and adoption thresholds. If successful, the pilot can scale to additional courses with a proven model.

agent_task.prompt:
Create a 12-week strategic launch plan for a pilot AI course-QA assistant at a university department.

Scenario: The pilot will support two undergraduate courses with about 300 total students. The team has one part-time faculty sponsor, two student developers, one teaching assistant, and a $5,000 pilot budget. The assistant can answer questions only from instructor-approved course materials and must include safeguards for hallucination, privacy, academic-integrity concerns, and escalation to human staff.

The plan should define phases, milestones, owners, success metrics, risks, and decision gates for whether to expand the pilot after 12 weeks.

Do not invent university policies or completed pilot results. Clearly label assumptions, keep the plan within the $5,000 budget, and restrict assistant answers to instructor-approved materials.
